claims.claim_text: The opening scenario says a staff engineer is paged at 02:10 because checkout latency crossed the customer-facing SLO for the third time in a week.
claims.anchor_text: A staff engineer is paged at 02:10 because checkout latency has crossed the customer-facing SLO for the third time in a week.
claims.suggested_rewrite: A staff engineer is paged because checkout latency has crossed the customer-facing SLO again.
claims.rationale: This is a precise incident-style teaching anecdote with time and recurrence details but no attributable real source, so the rewrite removes fake precision while preserving the operational setup.

claims.claim_text: Datadog Watchdog provides automatic anomaly detection and surfaces anomaly stories with impact and root-cause context.
claims.rationale: Datadog's official Watchdog Alerts documentation directly describes proactive anomaly detection and anomaly context, but docs.datadoghq.com is outside the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Dynatrace Davis/Dynatrace Intelligence performs topology-aware root cause and impact analysis using Smartscape, OneAgent, and dependency context.
claims.rationale: Dynatrace's official root-cause-analysis documentation directly describes context-aware analysis across topology, services, hosts, transactions, Smartscape, OneAgent, and impact, but docs.dynatrace.com is not allowlisted.

claims.claim_text: New Relic Applied Intelligence/AIOps supports alert correlation, noise reduction, causal context, and incident workflow automation.
claims.rationale: New Relic's official Applied Intelligence page directly discusses anomaly detection, alert correlation, noise reduction, root-cause context, workflows, and incident lifecycle features, but newrelic.com is not on the allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Splunk ITSI provides service modeling, service/KPI health, and Event Analytics for notable event grouping into episodes.
claims.rationale: Splunk's official ITSI Event Analytics documentation supports event ingestion, service/KPI context, and notable-event aggregation, but docs.splunk.com is not allowlisted.

claims.claim_text: Elastic machine learning supports anomaly detection, outlier detection, and forecasting over Elastic data.
claims.rationale: Elastic's official machine-learning documentation directly describes anomaly detection, outlier detection, and forecasting, but elastic.co is outside the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Grafana AI includes assistant workflows, query help, AI-powered investigations, incident summaries, anomaly detection, and forecasting.
claims.rationale: Grafana's official AI documentation lists Grafana Assistant, anomaly detection and forecasting, Sift investigations, incident auto-summary, and dashboard metadata generation.

claims.claim_text: The module says Datadog is often strongest for broad automatic detection across cloud-native telemetry with minimal setup.
claims.anchor_text: Datadog is often strongest when a team wants broad automatic detection across cloud-native telemetry with minimal setup.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Datadog may fit teams that want broad automatic detection across cloud-native telemetry with minimal setup.
claims.rationale: This is an uncited comparative superlative rather than a directly sourced benchmark; the rewrite removes the ranking while preserving selection guidance.

claims.claim_text: The module says Dynatrace is often strongest for complex environments where dependency topology and deterministic analysis matter more than time-window grouping.
claims.anchor_text: Dynatrace is often strongest when the environment is complex enough that dependency topology and deterministic analysis are more valuable than simple time-window grouping.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Dynatrace may fit environments where dependency topology and deterministic analysis are more valuable than simple time-window grouping.
claims.rationale: This is an uncited comparative superlative rather than a directly sourced benchmark; the rewrite removes the ranking while preserving selection guidance.

claims.claim_text: The module says New Relic is often strongest when the operational problem is too many alerts and not enough incident structure.
claims.anchor_text: New Relic is often strongest when the operational problem is too many alerts and not enough incident structure.
claims.suggested_rewrite: New Relic may fit teams whose operational problem is too many alerts and not enough incident structure.
claims.rationale: This is an uncited comparative superlative rather than a directly sourced benchmark; the rewrite removes the ranking while preserving selection guidance.

claims.claim_text: The module says Splunk ITSI is often strongest where an organization already models business services and runs a large Splunk estate.
claims.anchor_text: Splunk ITSI is often strongest where the organization already models business services and runs a large Splunk estate.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Splunk ITSI may fit organizations that already model business services and run a large Splunk estate.
claims.rationale: This is an uncited comparative superlative rather than a directly sourced benchmark; the rewrite removes the ranking while preserving selection guidance.

claims.claim_text: In Kubernetes, a container that continues to consume memory beyond its memory limit can be terminated and reported as OOMKilled.
claims.rationale: Kubernetes documentation directly explains that a container exceeding its memory limit may be terminated and shows OOMKilled output.

claims.claim_text: Logs can contain sensitive information and therefore need security-conscious management and access controls.
claims.rationale: NIST SP 800-92 is authoritative guidance for computer security log management and supports treating log data as security-sensitive operational data.

further_reading.title: Introduction to Grafana AI
further_reading.why_relevant: Provides allowlisted official grounding for Grafana Assistant, anomaly detection, forecasting, Sift, and AI incident summaries.

further_reading.title: OpenTelemetry Traces
further_reading.why_relevant: Explains the trace and span evidence that observability AI features should expose when making causation claims.

further_reading.title: Assign Memory Resources to Containers and Pods
further_reading.why_relevant: Grounds the Kubernetes OOMKilled and memory-limit behavior used in the module's incident examples.

further_reading.title: NIST SP 800-92: Guide to Computer Security Log Management
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful background for treating logs and telemetry as sensitive operational data that needs governance.

notes: Vendor primary documentation for Datadog, Dynatrace, New Relic, Splunk, and Elastic is the right evidence for their product-capability claims, but those hosts are outside the current trusted-domain allowlist, so those claims are marked needs_allowlist_expansion. Quiz prompts, code blocks, and exercise outputs were intentionally not cited unless they stated a real platform behavior outside the exercise mechanics.
